markets.title: Above 5.8%
markets.race_title: Above 5.8%
markets.context: VoteHub generic ballot polling (last 5 polls) shows Democrats at 44.4% and Republicans at 42.2%, a Democratic spread of 2.2 points on average. Recent spread readings vary by 3.0 points across the sample.
markets.analysis: Marcus applies the same bracket heuristic to the Democratic spread and estimates fair value at 10¢ for this binary market. Versus the current price of 56¢, the model implies a tradable edge.

markets.title: Will the President's approval rating be above 41.6 according to RealClearPolitics?
markets.race_title: Will the President's approval rating be above 41.6 according to RealClearPolitics?
markets.context: VoteHub approval polling (last 5 polls) shows average approval at 40.4% and disapproval at 56.6%. Recent approval readings span a 8.0-point range, indicating the current noise band.
markets.analysis: This market resolves on a binary threshold from the listed bracket, and Marcus maps the current polling level to an estimated fair value of 35¢ under the heuristic model. Compared with the current market price of 2¢, the model signals a tradable edge.

markets.title: Who will win Los Angeles Mayoral Election?
markets.race_title: Who will win Los Angeles Mayoral Election?
markets.context: LA Mayor race polling (Race to the WH average, April 2026) shows Karen Bass leading at 23%, followed by Nithya Raman (13%) and Spencer Pratt (12%). 42% of voters remain undecided. The primary is June 2, 2026 — 10 candidate contracts active. Fundraising: Bass $28.5M, Miller $27.2M.
markets.analysis: Marcus evaluates the full race field. Leading candidate Karen Bass (FV 31¢) is still below majority support, so the model treats this as a volatile top-two/runoff race rather than a clean incumbent lock. Contracts with large poll-to-price gaps represent edge. Note: 42% undecided/other voters make this race volatile — a runoff is likely (top two vote-getters advance to November).

sources.label: Race to the WH — LA Mayor polling
